---
title: "Gruvbox Theme for Quarto"
subtitle: "A retro-groove HTML theme, dark and light"
author: "Roman Overko"
date: last-modified
format:
  gruvbox-html: default
---

## Introduction

[Gruvbox](https://github.com/morhetz/gruvbox) theme for Quarto HTML. Two
variants, dark and light, both covering the same set of components: navigation,
callouts, tables, code blocks, the table of contents, and tabsets.

The theme follows the reader's system preference by default. Enable the toggle
in `_quarto.yml` to let readers switch manually from the navbar.

## Typography

Body text is set in [Roboto Mono](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto+Mono).
The usual markdown works: **bold**, *italic*, `inline code`, ~~strikethrough~~,
and [links](https://quarto.org).

### Headings

Headings use a lighter foreground tone. `<h1>` gets a thin bottom border
to break up the page.

## Lists

Ordered:

1. Backgrounds: `bg`, `bg-hard`, `bg-soft`, `bg1`-`bg4`
2. Foregrounds: `fg1`-`fg4`, `gray`
3. Accents: `red`, `green`, `yellow`, `blue`, `purple`, `aqua`, `orange`

Nested unordered:

- Dark theme uses the `bright` accent family
  - Bright variants stay readable on the dark background
- Light theme uses the `faded` accent family
  - Faded variants have enough weight on the cream background

## Blockquote

> Retro groove colour scheme for Vim.
>
> — Pavel Pertsev

## Code

Inline code uses strong yellow on a soft background. Block code picks up
the Gruvbox syntax theme: red keywords, green strings, gray italic
comments, purple numbers, yellow types, orange operators, blue variables.
Colors swap to the correct variant (bright on dark, faded on light) with
the theme.

A Python block:

```python
def gruvbox_palette():
    return {
        "red":    "#cc241d",
        "green":  "#98971a",
        "yellow": "#d79921",
        "blue":   "#458588",
        "purple": "#b16286",
        "aqua":   "#689d6a",
        "orange": "#d65d0e",
    }
```

And an R block:

```r
colors <- c("#cc241d", "#98971a", "#d79921",
            "#458588", "#b16286", "#689d6a", "#d65d0e")
barplot(rep(1, length(colors)), col = colors, border = NA, axes = FALSE)
```

## Tables

| Variant | Background | Foreground | Accent family |
|--------:|:-----------|:-----------|:--------------|
| Dark    | `#282828`  | `#ebdbb2`  | bright        |
| Light   | `#fbf1c7`  | `#3c3836`  | faded         |

## Figures

```{=html}
<figure style="text-align: center;">
  <svg width="420" height="60" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
    <rect x="0"   y="0" width="60" height="60" fill="#cc241d"/>
    <rect x="60"  y="0" width="60" height="60" fill="#98971a"/>
    <rect x="120" y="0" width="60" height="60" fill="#d79921"/>
    <rect x="180" y="0" width="60" height="60" fill="#458588"/>
    <rect x="240" y="0" width="60" height="60" fill="#b16286"/>
    <rect x="300" y="0" width="60" height="60" fill="#689d6a"/>
    <rect x="360" y="0" width="60" height="60" fill="#d65d0e"/>
  </svg>
  <figcaption>Gruvbox accents: red, green, yellow, blue, purple, aqua, orange.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

## Math

Inline: $c \geq 4.5$ is the body-text contrast target.

Display:

$$
E = mc^2 \qquad\Longleftrightarrow\qquad
\nabla \cdot \mathbf{E} = \frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0}
$$

## Callouts

Each of the five callout types uses its canonical Gruvbox color for the
border, header tint, and icon.

::: {.callout-note}
Blue. Side notes, supplementary context.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
Green. Suggestions and shortcuts.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
Yellow. Worth checking before you continue.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
Purple. Don't skip.
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
Red. Destructive or irreversible.
:::

## Tabsets

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Dark palette

| Role   | Hex                |
|--------|--------------------|
| bg     | `#282828`          |
| fg     | `#ebdbb2`          |
| yellow | `#fabd2f` (bright) |
| blue   | `#83a598` (bright) |

### Light palette

| Role   | Hex               |
|--------|-------------------|
| bg     | `#fbf1c7`         |
| fg     | `#3c3836`         |
| yellow | `#b57614` (faded) |
| blue   | `#076678` (faded) |

:::

## CSS variables

Every palette entry is also exposed as a CSS custom property on `:root`,
so you can use it in inline HTML:

```css
.my-widget {
  background: var(--bg-soft);
  color:      var(--fg);
  border:     1px solid var(--yellow-strong);
}
```

`--*-strong` points at the bright family in the dark theme and the faded
family in the light theme. One rule, both modes.

## References

- Gruvbox: <https://github.com/morhetz/gruvbox>
- Quarto HTML options: <https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/html-basics.html>
